content:
# Monthly Food Safety Quick Audit

**Tool ID:** T017-01
**Block:** B017 (Internal Food Safety Self-Assessment)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** Manager
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It mirrors what a VMVT inspector actually checks, so you find the problems first.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because the details it covers are the ones that slip when you are busy -- and the ones that matter most when they do.

**Built for Manager working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Simplified monthly audit checklist covering key inspection criteria: food storage, preparation, cooking, hygiene, cleaning, documentation, structure, and management controls. Score yourself honestly -- this is your early warning system.

This tool supports **Internal Food Safety Self-Assessment** (B017) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** Manager
**Time Required:** 15-20 minutes
**Frequency:** Monthly -- schedule for the same week each month (e.g., first Monday).

**What You Will Need:**
- This checklist (printed or on tablet)
- Probe thermometer (calibrated)
- Access to all storage areas, kitchen, and service counter
- Previous month audit for comparison

**Steps:**
1. Walk the entire premises -- do not audit from the office
2. Score each item: PASS (fully compliant), PARTIAL (minor issue), FAIL (non-compliant)
3. For PARTIAL or FAIL, write the specific issue and corrective action
4. Calculate your section scores and overall percentage
5. Compare with last month -- are you improving?
6. Brief the team on findings within 24 hours

---

## Checklist

### Section 1: Food Storage (Target: 100% pass)

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 1.1 | Fridge temperature reading now: _____ deg C (target 1-5 deg C) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.2 | Freezer temperature reading now: _____ deg C (target -18 deg C or below) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.3 | All items in fridge are covered, labelled, and date-coded | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.4 | Raw and cooked foods separated (raw below cooked) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.5 | No out-of-date items in any fridge, freezer, or dry store | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.6 | FIFO (first in, first out) is being followed | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.7 | Dry store clean, tidy, items off the floor on shelving | P | Pa | F | | |
| 1.8 | Milk stored at 1-5 deg C, within use-by date | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 1 Score:** _____ / 8 Pass

### Section 2: Food Preparation and Cooking

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 2.1 | Separate chopping boards used for raw / cooked / allergen-free | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.2 | Probe thermometer available and calibrated (ice water test: 0 deg C +/- 1) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.3 | Cooked food reaches core temperature of 75 deg C+ | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.4 | Hot-held food maintained at 63 deg C+ (check now: _____ deg C) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.5 | Cooling procedures followed (hot food cooled to below 8 deg C within 90 min) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 2.6 | No evidence of cross-contamination risk in prep area | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 2 Score:** _____ / 6 Pass

### Section 3: Cleaning and Disinfection

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 3.1 | Cleaning schedule displayed and up to date | P | Pa | F | | |
| 3.2 | All food contact surfaces clean to touch and sight | P | Pa | F | | |
| 3.3 | Correct sanitiser dilution used (check chemical data sheets) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 3.4 | Dishwasher / glasswasher reaching 82 deg C+ rinse cycle | P | Pa | F | | |
| 3.5 | Waste bins clean, lined, and not overflowing | P | Pa | F | | |
| 3.6 | Espresso machine group heads and steam wand visibly clean | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 3 Score:** _____ / 6 Pass

### Section 4: Personal Hygiene and Staff Practices

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 4.1 | Handwashing sink stocked (soap, paper towels, warm water) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.2 | Staff observed washing hands at correct times | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.3 | Clean uniforms / aprons worn, hair tied back | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.4 | No jewellery policy followed (watches, rings removed) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 4.5 | Staff aware of fitness-to-work rules (can name 2+ exclusion symptoms) | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 4 Score:** _____ / 5 Pass

### Section 5: Allergen Management

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 5.1 | Allergen matrix displayed and matches current menu | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.2 | Staff can name at least 5 of the 14 EU allergens unprompted | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.3 | Allergen query procedure known (ask 1 team member to demonstrate) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 5.4 | Allergen-free prep area or protocol in use | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 5 Score:** _____ / 4 Pass

### Section 6: Documentation and Records

| # | Audit Item | Pass | Partial | Fail | Issue Found | Corrective Action |
|---|-----------|------|---------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| 6.1 | Temperature logs complete for last 7 days (no gaps) | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.2 | Cleaning records complete for last 7 days | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.3 | All staff food hygiene certificates on file | P | Pa | F | | |
| 6.4 | Corrective actions from last audit completed | P | Pa | F | | |

**Section 6 Score:** _____ / 4 Pass

### Overall Score

| Section | Score | Max | % |
|---------|-------|-----|---|
| 1. Food Storage | | 8 | |
| 2. Preparation and Cooking | | 6 | |
| 3. Cleaning | | 6 | |
| 4. Personal Hygiene | | 5 | |
| 5. Allergens | | 4 | |
| 6. Documentation | | 4 | |
| **TOTAL** | | **33** | |

**Rating Guide:**
- 90-100% (30-33 Pass): Excellent -- inspection ready
- 75-89% (25-29 Pass): Good -- minor actions needed within 7 days
- 60-74% (20-24 Pass): Requires Improvement -- action plan within 48 hours
- Below 60% (under 20 Pass): Urgent -- immediate corrective action, escalate to Owner

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Audited by: _______________
- Overall score: _____ / 33 ( _____%)
- Priority actions (top 3): 1. _____ 2. _____ 3. _____
- Next audit date: _______________

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Generous Self-Scoring

**Why it happens:** You audit your own venue and want it to look good. Partial becomes Pass. Minor issues get overlooked.
**How to prevent:** Ask another manager or the Head Barista to co-audit. Two pairs of eyes are harder to fool.
**If it is already happening:** Compare your self-score to the last official VMVT inspection. If there is a gap of more than 15%, your self-scoring is too generous.

### Risk 2: Audit Happens But Actions Do Not

**Why it happens:** The checklist gets completed, filed, and forgotten. Same issues appear next month.
**How to prevent:** Transfer the top 3 actions to T017-03 (Action Plan Template). Set deadlines and assign owners. Review at next team meeting.
**If it is already happening:** Pull the last 3 audits. List recurring fails. These are systemic -- they need a process change, not just a reminder.

### Risk 3: Only Auditing During Quiet Periods

**Why it happens:** Manager waits for a calm afternoon to audit. Everything looks great because there is no food out, no rush pressure.
**How to prevent:** Audit during or immediately after a rush (08:30-09:30 or 12:00-13:00). That is when real practices are visible.
**If it is already happening:** Schedule your next audit for a Tuesday morning at 09:00, mid-rush.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** Add items for specialty milk handling (oat, soy -- check allergen cross-contact) and single-origin coffee storage.
**Chain / Franchise:** Cross-reference against your brand audit scorecard. Add any brand-specific items not covered here.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Reduce to Sections 1, 4, and 5 as minimum. Add vehicle/stall cleanliness check.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add baking temperature checks, proving area hygiene, and display case rotation times.

**Making it yours:**
- Cross out items that do not apply (e.g., hot-hold if you do not hot-hold food)
- Add venue-specific items (e.g., outdoor seating cleanliness)
- Adjust scoring thresholds based on your inspection history
- Share monthly trend with the whole team -- transparency drives improvement

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T017-02 -- Quarterly Comprehensive Food Safety Audit (L2, audit)
- T017-03 -- Self-Assessment Action Plan Template (L1, template)
- T017-04 -- FHRS Rating Trend Tracker (L3, dashboard)
- T017-05 -- Self-Assessment SOP (L2, SOP)

**Related blocks:**
- B016

**Parent block:** B017 -- Internal Food Safety Self-Assessment
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
